tool.name: google_dork
tool.description:
Generate Google dork queries for passive OSINT reconnaissance. Google dorks reveal sensitive files, admin panels, exposed configs, git repos, and more without directly scanning the target.
Preset types: admin_panels, sensitive_files, exposed_docs, api_endpoints, config_files, error_pages, backup_files, git_exposure, open_dirs, subdomains, passwords, database
Examples: - google_dork(domain="example.com", dork_type="all") — generate all 12 dork queries - google_dork(domain="example.com", dork_type="git_exposure") — check for exposed .git - google_dork(domain="example.com", custom_dork="site:example.com ext:php inurl:upload")
Execute returned search_urls via browser or web_search tool.

tool.name: check_breaches
tool.description:
Check if a domain or email appears in known data breaches (HaveIBeenPwned, BreachDirectory). Useful for finding leaked credentials, understanding target's security history.
Examples: - check_breaches(domain="example.com") — check if domain was in breaches - check_breaches(email="admin@example.com") — check specific email

tool.name: osint_recon
tool.description:
Passive OSINT reconnaissance — gathers intelligence WITHOUT touching the target directly. Checks: Certificate Transparency logs, DNS records, robots.txt, security.txt, email patterns.
Use this early in the scan to build intelligence before active testing.
